rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ADAM NUNZIATO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Previously Peremptory Challenged Hon. Charles F. Haines' Two Void March 12, 2024 Orders
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 3, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE KORMAN FAMILY IRREVOCABLE TRUST, NANCY E. RYTI, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Previously Peremptory Challenged Hon. Charles F. Haines' Two Void March 12, 2024 Orders is DENIED. The rulings reflected in the March 12, 2024 formal order were made in the minute orders on November 17, 2022 and January 13, 2023 before the challenge. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. ROBERT WRIGHT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 3, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ROBERT WRIGHT Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is GRANTED in part and Hearing Required in part. The Court finds that Plaintiff breached the stipulation by providing a reference that was not neutral. Hearing Required to address the remedy. Parties to appear in-person at 8:30 a.m. on July 3, 2024 and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA VS. ROBERT LAPIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 3, 2024 line 9. DEFENDANT LYNETTE BREMER DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations in the complaint and with exhibits: (1) paragraph 6d and Ex. 4; and (2) paragraph 10 and Ex. 3. If the optional form complaint does not provide sufficient options to the Plaintiff to properly plead the elements of the cause of action, Plaintiff may file a regular pleading format complaint or avail itself of the options provided for in CCP 1166 for pleading such requirements. Demurrer is otherwise OVERRULED. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
